name: bruteforce-salted-openssl
title: bruteforce-salted-openssl
summary: try to find the passphrase for files encrypted with OpenSSL
description:
try to find the passphrase for files encrypted with OpenSSL
bruteforce-salted-openssl try to find the passphrase or password of a file
that was encrypted with the openssl command. It can be used in two ways:
.
- Try all possible passwords given a charset.
- Try all passwords in a file (dictionary).
.
bruteforce-salted-openssl have the following features:
.
- You can specify the number of threads to use when cracking a file.
- The program should be able to use all the digests and symmetric ciphers
available with the OpenSSL libraries installed on your system.
- Sending a USR1 signal to a running bruteforce-salted-openssl process
makes it print progress and continue.
- There are an exhaustive mode and a dictionary mode.
.
In the exhaustive mode the program tries to decrypt the file by trying all
possible passwords. It is especially useful if you know something about the
password (i.e. you forgot a part of your password but still remember most of
it). Finding the password of the file without knowing anything about it would
take way too much time (unless the password is really short and/or weak).
There are some command line options to specify:
.
- The minimum password length to try.
- The maximum password length to try.
- The beginning of the password.
- The end of the password.
- The character set to use (among the characters of the current locale).
.
In dictionary mode the program tries to decrypt the file by trying all the
passwords contained in a file. The file must have one password per line.
.
This package is useful for security, pentests and forensics investigations.

arguments.raw: -c  Cipher for decryption. The default value is aes-256-cbc.

arguments.description: End of the password. Default: "".

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use bruteforce-salted-openssl because try to find the passphrase for files encrypted with OpenSSL.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use bruteforce-salted-openssl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bruteforce-salted-openssl [options] <filename>` as documented upstream for bruteforce-salted-openssl.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bruteforce-salted-openssl -t 4 -l 5 -m 5 -c aes256 encrypted.file` as documented upstream for bruteforce-salted-openssl.

When you need a concrete invocation, run `bruteforce-salted-openssl -t 8 -l 9 -m 11 -c des3 -b "AbCD" -e "Ef" -s "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz" encrypted.file` as documented upstream for bruteforce-salted-openssl.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bruteforce-salted-openssl -t 6 -f dictionary.txt -c aes256 encrypted-file` as documented upstream for bruteforce-salted-openssl.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair bruteforce-salted-openssl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bruteforce-salted-openssl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: bruteforce-salted-openssl - try to find the passphrase for files encrypted with OpenSSL
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install bruteforce-salted-openssl. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `bruteforce-salted-openssl [options] <filename>`. Further invocations from upstream: `bruteforce-salted-openssl -t 4 -l 5 -m 5 -c aes256 encrypted.file`; `bruteforce-salted-openssl -t 8 -l 9 -m 11 -c des3 -b "AbCD" -e "Ef" -s "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz" encrypted.file`. try to find the passphrase for files encrypted with OpenSSL.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: try to find the passphrase for files encrypted with OpenSSL.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: bruteforce-salted-openssl - try to find the passphrase for files encrypted with OpenSSL.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install bruteforce-salted-openssl
Install with: sudo apt install bruteforce-salted-openssl
